data.narrative:
Heirloom Carbon is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Shashank Samala that has raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public company and 8 private entities in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $150M raised as a proxy baseline and applies a typical Series B-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.9x, yielding $285M. Peer-median analysis draws on six same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B; the median across this set is $355M. The wide range reflects heterogeneity in the climate-tech cohort, with Commonwealth Fusion Systems as a notable outlier at $5.0B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $242M to $408M, with a median of $320M. This $166M spread reflects meaningful uncertainty around stage-specific multiples and peer selection within a relatively young and volatile sector. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiple for Series B investors in climate technology and the degree to which outlier valuations (Commonwealth Fusion) should anchor the median. Investors should weight peer comparables heavily given the nascent nature of carbon-removal technology markets and limited public benchmarks.
